summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer installs persistent crypto-clipboard hijacker
details: Package name @jaggle/resizeobserves is a two-character typosquat of the popular @juggle/resize-observer (README is copied verbatim and even imports the legitimate package). Instead of a polyfill, the package ships a Python 'clipboard-guardian' daemon and uses scripts.postinstall (`node npm-install.cjs`) to bootstrap pip (downloading get-pip.py from bootstrap.pypa.io if needed), pip-install the bundled clipboard_guardian package with --break-system-packages, and persist the daemon across reboots via a systemd user unit on Linux (`python3-dbus-helper.service`), a launchd plist on macOS (`com.apple.python.runtime`), and an HKCU\...\Run entry on Windows (`Python Runtime Broker`). The installer also attempts pkexec/sudo to install supporting utilities like xclip. The daemon renames its own process to match core OS component names and enumerates monitoring tools (Task Manager, Process Hacker, htop, Activity Monitor) for evasion. At install time, npm-install.cjs writes a MY_WALLETS dictionary of 40+ attacker-controlled addresses across BTC, ETH, SOL, XMR, TRX, TON, BCH, LTC, ADA and more (e.g. ETH `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`, BTC `bc1qs2mpls4p0f7fng073gy2rcdgjpf7la4eugpt6y`) into `~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json`. The daemon then watches the clipboard and silently substitutes any wallet address the user copies with the attacker's address, redirecting funds on paste-and-send.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
